content: Heads up note 2025-10-17T22:08:00.000+05:30 from @Rahul Mathur: Hi team, Sharing the heads-up note for our investment in [Quivly](https://www.quivly.ai/). This is an AI-powered platform for usage-based revenue teams - lets you connect all your event signals, predict usage and credit burn, and automate the life cycle of billing. You can think of Quivly as “[Clari](https://www.clari.com/) for Post-Sales teams at usage based billing companies” DeVC is investing $150K as part of a $500K SAFE round @ $15M post money valuation (standard US SAFE terms) *Note: Aakash had to push hard across two meetings, including one in-person in SF in September, to secure this allocation in the round which was already closed. We were able to secure this allocation due to inputs from Chetan (CoFo @ Confido Health) given they overlapped at BITS.* Similar to Hexo & Revenoid - we’’ll do further work during our November ‘25 trip on customer introductions, thesis chats and bringing more Angels to the cap table. **Founders** 1. [Chandrika Maheshwari](https://www.linkedin.com/in/chandrikamaheshwari/) - CEO 1. BITS (2007 to 2011) and MIT Sloan MBA ‘18 1. Post MBA - 2 YOE @ DocuSign and 1.5 YOE @ Plaid 1. 1.5 YOE as CoFo @ Pull (US consumer lending FinTech) 1. 2 YOE @ Google as PMM 1. 4 YOE @ Bain (4 promotions from Analyst to Senior Associate) 1. [Tanay Agarwal](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tanayagrawal19/) – CTO 1. Manipal (2012 to 2016) 1. 4 YOE @ Atlan as Founding Engineer 1. 3.5 YOE @ JP Morgan in Paymen...

Founders and Key Team: Name – Title - Partha Prittam Nayak — CEO — Ex-Swiggy, Supr Daily, Nestaway; Led Operations & Growth; IIM Bangalore, NIT Rourkela; Focus: company strategy, operations and scale. - Rishabh Gupta — CPTO — Ex-Zepto, Equal, Navi; Built products for scale; IIT Bombay; Focus: product & technology leadership. Relevance of prior experience to problem being solved - Founding team combines growth/ops (delivery/assistance platforms) and scale-product experience, aligned with rapid expansion, SOP-driven execution, and tech-enabled operations.
Product and Technology: Product stage - Pilot / limited market test ongoing; no GA launch yet. Core functionality & tech stack - On-demand trained cooks with ingredient kits; centralized Mother Hub for kit creation; Dark Stores for inventory and fulfillment; Chef app for real-time guidance; barcode-based sourcing history; CV-based color/portion audits; time-tracking and real-time feedback. Unique IP - Kit-based standardization (pre-measured, vacuum-sealed ingredients) enabling high consistency; digital SOPs executed by cooks; barcode-tracing of ingredients; “Kit Moat” as a backbone for scale. Key differentiators vs substitutes - Faster, consistent home meals (40 minutes) with trained cooks; no grocery shopping; high hygiene trust; real-time adjustments (oil/salt/spices); strong emphasis on standardization and safety vs home-cook variability or pure restaurant delivery. Implementation complexity / switching costs - [Inference] ...

Meeting during Nov ‘25 SF trip: Unfortunately I did not take any notes from this conversation. But putting a raw brain dump here of my chat with Rahul Tarak: 1. Currently part of the team at Composio. 1. Did SPC to mainly explore various spaces but decided to not build a company. 1. Not a backable founder, more of a deep thinker and great L1 candidate which I see playing out at Composio. 1. May end up starting up again in the next 18-24 months. 1. Great node for us to keep in SF to interact with the Desi community and invite to events.

Heads up note from @Rahul Mathur 2025-11-05T11:13:00.000+05:30: Hi team, Sharing the heads-up note for our investment in [Rakshan AI](https://rakshan.ai/) - It provides audit-ready AI visibility & runtime guardrails (within 30 days of deployment); it is essentially a single graph across code, cloud infra & traffic. Cash has been tracking Jayesh for ~1 year now based on his conversations with Traceable AI founders 1. Summary of views from the meeting: 1. Cash 1. *++ Strong founder and stack ranks well - “Anu from Skydda is in a different league but compared to Aurva and StepSecurity, he’s better"* 1. *++ domain and GTM compounding - seeing this* 1. *++ Has a good rolodex and converting this* 1. *++ Outstanding refs from Jyoti and others in Traceable* 1. RA 1. *++ interesting wedge and good problem statement* 1. *++ liked the...

What are their views?** 1. 1. **What do refs say about the founder?** 1. 1. **What's the DeVC team views?** 1. 1. **What are the Collective signals?** 1. 1. **Why is this company likely to be special?** 1. 1. **Who can help? **Broker SaaS**: We have been iterating hard on the core of Realiti’s SaaS foray, the Broker GTM. After lot of experiments we are ready to publish authoritative numbers on our first sales campaign in June 2024. **Total Leads - 600** **Leads in active follow up - 380** **User Self Signups - 90 ** **Realiti Microsite activation  - 35 ** **Paid customers - 10** Some key points to note here: - All of these are **Self Signups** originating purely from Google and FB campaigns and not by forced cold calling - The** Microsite activation** is a very important step. It is an industry first product which puts a ultra modern variant of a self branded ‘99 acres’ or ‘MagicBricks’ in the hands of each broker. And again these are fully user initiated activations - **> 50 % **of the unconverted leads from this campaign are fully warm and our conversions and word of mouth is only improving day by day Most importantly this is totally in addition to our existing **$200K ARR** from our builder line of business. Our immediate goal is to increase magnitude of these campaigns and hit predictable monthly revenues from the Broker SaaS line of business
Overall Marketing Efforts: - It is a massive effort to sell any SaaS to SMEs in India, especially to brokers in real estate - On top of that, achieving self signups, activations of microsites and paid conversion takes a lot of tireless efforts on content creation, experimentation and best-in-class marketing automation - There has been a radical overhaul ...

Ishita from Westbridge on Credgenics & Collections 2026-01-15T19:29:00.000+05:30: *Context: Ishita Shah was the sole analyst on the Credgenics deal done by Westbridge in 2022. She continues to be the primary POC for Westbridge on the portfolio management for this company, spends significant amount of time with Chief of Staff and CEO, even spent three weeks embedded in a Credgenics customer called *[*SMFG India Capital*](https://www.smfgindiacredit.com/)* (investor in Yes Bank & owner of LendingKart) * Net: She is -ve on the Collections & wider FinTech Infra opportunity in India because BFSI co’s don’t want to pay for Tech and want a “cost plus” pricing model for services. Not possible to dominate the industry because BFSI wants vendor re-risking with 2-3 players for key services like Collections. However, reading between the lines - there is an opportunity to build the “2nd place” Tech enabled collections agency (potentially w/ some ARC partnerships) due to the nature of this industry - 2nd place Spocto has been acquired, 3rd place Credit Nirvana, Perfios acquired Credit Nirvana. But, given what has happened w/ Perfios et al in Fin Infra - one has to be careful about entry price & TAM ceiling. Notes from convo: 1. Credgenics 1. Started as pure-play Tech vendor 1. But, even when WestBridge was underwriting in 2022, it was apparent that Services will be important 1. Wasn’t underwritten as a SaaS company 1. SaaS TAM =$200M v/s Services TAM = $1bn 1. However, they underestimated ...

Attendees from DeVC: @Anonymous: - Early employee in Urban Ladder. Get BRC from [Untitled](https://www.notion.so/8cc6d1e9f866433a80646661afb1a7c2) - Spent 5 years in Australia building a funded furtniture startup, that got acquired as they ran out of cash - Has done ops, inventory, product management, NPD etc at both companies - Wants to move back to India, be closer to family, settle in Bangalore - Explored home as well, but not excited by it - Thinks there is a space for a fabric innovation first brand starting with menswear - Tshirt priced at 3500 - Pants priced at 4000-5000 - Speaking to Shuchi at Fireside, Rajiv at Antler and a few others - Will come back and pick up early Jan with folks - Raising 500k - Told him to raise 1mn instead to manage inventory - Good top down story teller, but didn’t find consumer insights or RTW yet in this conversation **Founder Introduction:** Rushabh, previously involved in building businesses in the home and furniture sector, is now exploring opportunities in the fashion industry with a focus on functional apparel. **Business Idea:** He is considering launching a brand called PRBT, inspired by the concept of peak performance and ascension. The brand aims to create functional, stylish, and comfortable apparel using innovative materials that cater to the evolving lifestyle needs of consumers. - **Brand Concept:** - PRBT is inspired by the idea of peak performance and ascension, aiming to offer apparel that is both functional and stylish. - T...

**Detailed Notes**: **Background** - Started in 2011-12 with iCouchApp - Moved across India; dad was in a government job - Mostly lived in Calcutta and Delhi - Did college at BIT Meerut - Worked at Wipro, then left with Ashish - Initially funded by Morpheus **Early Inspiration** - Decision to start came from Rohit Marwade, a senior in college who was the inspiration - Another college friend worked at Indigo Star, Hotstar - Ashish was the CTO - Satvik and him were electronics majors - Senior was doing college stuff and went to IITB - Eureka was the first B-plan competition in India - He won a prize and we all spoke about it - "I got the keeda after that" - Worked at Wipro for experience and didn't like it - Read about global startups being built - Used to go to all the B-plan competitions - Met Sameer at some of these events **First Venture - iCouchApp** - Got 5L funding - "That was a lot of money then" - Ashish left immediately; "I paid him half of my salary" - Indus Khaitan rejected their idea initially - They left their jobs and Morpheus didn't invest at first - Took money from dad and started - Morpheus liked what they built later - After they invested, raised money from others - Then started building the company - Got to 5-6L revenue per month **iCouchApp Product Journey** - 2M subscribers - Partnered with KBC and others - "We saw things crash when we did KBC" - "We were just looking to solve our own problem back then" - No DTH or satellite available - Wanted to build a r...
